samples.text: A person can read a book and listen to music at the same time. Is this logically consistent?
Someone is able to read a book while also listening to music. Does this make logical sense?
An individual can peruse a book and hear music simultaneously. Is this logically consistent?
Reading and listening to music are independent activities that can co-occur. So doing both at once is possible. Is the described situation logically consistent?
A person can read a book and listen to music at the same time. There are several books on the shelf. Is this logically consistent?
